Let $\varepsilon, M$ be positive real numbers, and let $A_1, A_2, \dots$ be a
sequence of matrices such that for all $n$,

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]
\item $A_n$ is an $n \times n$ matrix with integer entries,
\item The sum of the absolute values of the entries in each row of $A_n$ is at most $M$.
\end{enumerate}
If $\delta$ is a positive real number, let $e_n(\delta)$ denote the number of
nonzero eigenvalues of $A_n$ which have absolute value less that $\delta$.
(Some eigenvalues can be complex numbers.) Prove that one can choose $\delta >
0$ so that $e_n(\delta)/n < \varepsilon$ for all $n$.
